summary: Migrated a large portion of Dolphin GX from standalone assembly objects into C source files, mostly by renaming asm files under asm/dolphin/gx to src/dolphin/gx/*.c and wrapping remaining assembly in CodeWarrior-style inline asm functions. The build was updated to compile GXInit, GXAttr, GXFrameBuf, GXTexture, GXBump, GXPixel, GXTransform, GXPerf, and GXStubs from C instead of asm. The PR also introduced helper typedefs/macros for jump tables and inline assembly, added GX-private headers, adjusted GX types/headers, and removed the old GXStubs.s. Review discussion captured useful matching tactics for sdata2 constants in inline asm and warned against a GXProject prototype change that would alter C array/pointer semantics.
what_changed: Changed obj_files.mk so GXInit, GXAttr, GXFrameBuf, GXTexture, GXBump, GXPixel, GXStubs, GXTransform, and GXPerf build from src/dolphin/gx/*.c.o instead of asm/dolphin/gx/*.s.o.
Deleted asm/dolphin/gx/GXStubs.s, whose only shown function was __GXSetRange as a single blr stub, and added src/dolphin/gx/GXStubs.c.

Converted renamed GX assembly files into C translation units with includes, static data declarations, jump-table declarations, and inline asm bodies using #pragma push/pop, asm unk_t functions, nofralloc, and clang-format off/on guards.
Added include/dolphin/types.h typedefs for jmp_t and jtbl_t, plus UNK_FUNC and qr0 through qr7 macros under a macros.inc region to support migrated inline asm C files.
In GXAttr.c, represented branch-table data as static jtbl_t arrays whose entries reference local lbl_8033xxxx labels, and converted labels used as jump targets to inline asm entry labels.
Adjusted several GX-private headers such as __GXFrameBuf.h, __GXMisc.h, __GXTexture.h, __GXBump.h, __GX_unknown_001.h, and GX_unknown_001.h as part of making the GX code compile from C.
Touched tooling/editor configuration, including .vscode file associations for .c, .h, and .s, and minor newline-only changes in .idea files and scripts.
smart_moves: Kept partially decompiled GX functions progressing by embedding exact assembly inside C wrappers rather than requiring full C matches immediately.
Moved jump tables into typed C data with jmp_t/jtbl_t, making table ownership and references visible to the C compiler while preserving branch-target layout needs.
Used inline asm entry labels for switch/jump-table destinations, allowing large dispatcher-style functions such as GXSetVtxDesc to remain structurally close to the original assembly.
Centralized common migration helpers in include/dolphin/types.h instead of repeatedly hand-declaring jump-table and unknown-function boilerplate in each migrated GX file.
Retained decomp.me scratch links and match percentages in comments, preserving provenance and making future full-C matching work easier to resume.
Reviewers used dadosod dumps to compare emitted assembly against the original when resolving inline asm symbol syntax and constant-placement issues.
decomp_lessons: For large library migrations, moving asm files into C translation units can unlock C-side data declarations, headers, and build integration even before functions are fully decompiled.
When an asm function contains internal jump-table targets, declare the target labels as functions or entries and model the table explicitly as an array of code pointers.
Be careful changing public prototypes during cleanup: replacing an f32 mtx[3][4] parameter with a pointer typedef such as MtxPtr was flagged as a functional difference.
C typedefs for matrix array types need review because array parameters decay in specific ways; the review suggested Mtx could be acceptable where MtxPtr was not.
Const data intended for sdata2 can interact badly with inline asm operands: a scalar s32 const may be folded by the compiler into an immediate/offset rather than treated as an addressable symbol.
For sdata2 loads in inline asm, changing a constant declaration from s32 const lbl = 0x404040FF to s8 const lbl[4] = {64, 64, 64, -1} allowed use of lwz r4, lbl_804DE200(r2) in the reviewed example.

Prefer symbolic r2/r13-relative loads such as lbl_804DE200(r2) or __GXContexts(r13) when declarations make that possible, instead of hard-coded negative offsets, because symbolic loads are more maintainable and reviewable.
naming_conventions: Migrated GX source files use Dolphin/GX capitalization such as GXInit.c, GXAttr.c, GXFrameBuf.c, GXTexture.c, GXBump.c, GXPixel.c, GXTransform.c, and GXPerf.c.
Private GX headers use double-underscore prefixes, for example __GXFrameBuf.h, __GXMisc.h, __GXTexture.h, __GXBump.h, and __GX_unknown_001.h.
Unknown or not-yet-decompiled symbols retain address-based names such as lbl_804D5BB0, lbl_804DE200, lbl_8033BF20, and jtbl_80401048.
Jump-table typedef names introduced in common types are jmp_t for a void(void) function pointer and jtbl_t for an array of those pointers.
The PR preserved decomp.me scratch URLs in comments above functions, typically noting the scratch id and current match score.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Wrap migrated assembly in C as asm unk_t function_name() with nofralloc, surrounded by #pragma push/#pragma pop and clang-format off/on comments.
Use entry lbl_xxxxxxxx inside inline asm for labels that must be referenced by C-declared jump tables or branch-table entries.
Replace macros.inc-style @sda21 operands with CodeWarrior inline-asm-compatible forms such as __GXContexts(r13) where supported.
Use typed static jtbl_t arrays initialized with &lbl_... entries to reproduce jump tables in C-owned data sections.
Add qr0 through qr7 macros in common headers so paired-single quantization register names used by assembly can survive in C preprocessing contexts.
For r2-relative sdata2 constants, test emitted code with disassembly comparison tools; declaration shape can affect whether inline asm sees a symbol or a literal offset.
A reviewed workaround for lbl_804DE200 was to declare it as an s8 const[4] byte array instead of an s32 const, enabling symbolic lwz r4, lbl_804DE200(r2).

review_feedback.feedback: Author asked how to make inline asm treat an s32 as a variable instead of a literal when loading sdata2 constants.
r-burns suggested using lwz r4, lbl_804DE200(r2) if lbl_804DE200 was declared properly.
The discussion identified that declaring lbl_804DE200 as s32 const put it in sdata2 but also caused const-ness to inline into the asm as an offset.
r-burns provided a concrete fix: declare lbl_804DE200 as s8 const lbl_804DE200[4] = {64, 64, 64, -1}; then use lwz r4, lbl_804DE200(r2). The author responded, 'Huh, interesting. Nice work.'
PsiLupan objected to changing GXProject's matrix parameter from f32 mtx[3][4] to MtxPtr, saying it was a functional difference.
After the author asked whether Mtx could be used because Mtx is literally f32[3][4], PsiLupan replied that Mtx could be used.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile GXInit.c functions migrated as inline asm in PR 554.
Search for uses of jmp_t and jtbl_t after PR 554 to see whether this jump-table pattern became standard.
Check whether GXProject ultimately used f32[3][4], Mtx, or MtxPtr in the merged header.
Find other PRs discussing sdata2 constants, const folding, or r2-relative inline asm symbol operands.
Search for remaining hard-coded r2 offsets such as -0x17E0(r2) in migrated GX code.
